diagrams.title: Green IT Framework
diagrams.description: Shows the three pillars of Green IT and their components
elements: Environmental Sustainability (Energy efficiency, E-waste management, Carbon reduction)
Social Responsibility (CSR, Employee awareness, Community impact)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the holistic approach to Green IT implementation

diagrams.title: Data Center Energy Flow
diagrams.description: Illustrates how energy is distributed in a typical data center
elements: Total Facility Power Input → Power Distribution → IT Equipment (servers, storage, network) and Support Systems (cooling, lighting, security)
IT Equipment produces useful computation while Support Systems consume energy for maintenance
PUE = Total / IT Equipment - shows efficiency ratio
diagrams.purpose: Visualising PUE concept and energy optimisation opportunities

diagrams.title: IT Product Lifecycle
diagrams.description: Shows the stages of IT equipment from cradle to grave
elements: Manufacturing (raw materials, production) → Distribution → Usage (energy consumption, maintenance) → Disposal (e-waste) → Recycling (resource recovery) → Manufacturing (circular loop)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding where environmental impacts occur and how to minimise them

diagrams.title: Green IT Implementation Strategy
diagrams.description: Strategic approach for organisations adopting Green IT
elements: Assessment (current state analysis) → Planning (set goals, policies) → Implementation (deploy solutions) → Monitoring (measure metrics) → Optimisation (continuous improvement)
diagrams.purpose: Roadmap for successful Green IT adoption
